title: Trial Before Pilate
text:
The courtyard of the governor's palace. Pontius Pilate stands before the crowd, visibly reluctant. He has offered a release — Passover custom allows one prisoner to go free. The crowd must choose between Jesus and Barabbas, a man convicted of murder.

You are standing in that crowd.

The officials are working the edges — nudging people, hissing instructions. The crowd could go either way. You can feel it. You have one chance to influence this.

choices.text: Stay silent. Whatever the crowd decides, it was meant to be.
Shout 'Release Jesus!' as loudly as you can, hoping others follow your voice.
Move through the crowd — speak to individuals, change minds, organise resistance to the cry for Barabbas.
